title: Will AI talent departures hurt Alphabet's long-term growth?
description: Alphabet shares recorded their steepest one-day decline in more than a year after another prominent AI researcher departed Google DeepMind. The selloff wiped out over $225 billion in market value as investors questioned the company's ability to retain top artificial intelligence talent amid intensifying industry competition. (Sources: CNBC, Barron's, Bloomberg, Reuters) ​Alphabet Shares Tumble on AI Talent Concerns

title: Getty Images surges 145% after announcing OpenAI deal
description: The company’s shares have been hurt by the artificial intelligence “scare trade,” which has rippled through various corners of the market, from software and IT services firms to so-called “old economy” industries such as logistics
content:
Shares of stock-photo company Getty Images Holdings Inc. soared as much as 145 per cent on Monday after it announced a licensing deal with OpenAI.

title: SA government moves to regulate AI data centres as water, energy concerns mount
description: The South Australian government is pushing for the state to become home to billions of dollars' worth of artificial intelligence data centres, but says it will seek to regulate their water and energy use.
content:
The South Australian government is pushing for the state to become home to billions of dollars' worth of artificial intelligence (AI) data centres, but concerns have already been raised about the impact on energy and water supply.

title: Meta pauses AI training programme after internal data leak: Report
description: Meta has suspended an internal initiative that collected employee activity data to train artificial intelligence models after sensitive information was inadvertently made accessible across the company. The incident has reignited concerns over workplace surveillance, data governance and Meta's ability to secure AI systems amid a series of recent security setbacks.
content:
Meta has paused an internal artificial intelligence programme that gathered employee activity data after a security lapse exposed sensitive information to staff across the company, according to a report by Business Insider.

title: Petrol stations in the US accused of using AI to boost prices
description: A group of California consumers claimed in a lawsuit that petrol station owners including Walmart Inc, Marathon Petroleum Corp, BP Plc and 7-Eleven Inc are using artificial intelligence to illegally manipulate pump prices in the state that already has the highest rates in the US.

title: Sunshine Coast students push back as Australia positions itself as a global AI leader
description: As Australia tracks toward becoming a global leader in artificial intelligence infrastructure, a group of high school students is demanding stronger regulation.
content:
A $200 million data centre under construction on the Sunshine Coast has ignited a protest movement from students concerned about its environmental and social impacts.

title: AI Rewrites Oracle: 21,000 Jobs Cut As Cloud Wars Turn Fiercer, Tech Giants Double Down On Automation, Layoffs Signal New Rules; Will More People Lose Jobs To AI?
description: Oracle cuts 21,000 jobs as AI reshapes the workforce, signaling a major shift in the tech industry. The company’s restructuring reflects rising severance costs, cloud competition, and rapid AI adoption, while global tech giants continue heavy investment in artificial intelligence despite widespread layoffs.

title: Wikipedia won't let AI edit articles, co-founder Jimmy Wales says
description: Wikipedia does not trust artificial intelligence enough to let it play a direct role in editing articles on its platform, cofounder Jimmy Wales told AFP
